title: Best AI Trading Bots of 2026? My Plan for AI. (INSANE Profit)
author: DaviddTech
summary_bullets: Okay, let me take you back to January 2025.
CryptoBanta kindly invited me to be a host as their bot expert on their main channel.
And very quickly, one topic took over the room.
Bots vs humans, who would actually win?
So they put me on the spot.
They bet me that they could beat my trading bots with manual trading over a 30 day period.
Not knowing how competitive I was, I loaded 10k up their account with a mission in hand.
I was going to annihilate all manual trading and show them how good my bots were.
I didn't even make second place.
Embarrassing, yes I know, but here's the twist.

extracted_rules: I use ADX to stay out of markets when they go sideways.
And that's it, simple rules, automated execution.
They don't use the same logic and I'm very careful not to stack risk and I do my best to spread it.
Yes, I have a system to know when to turn off a bot.
Yes, I know it's a bit like when you're holding Bitcoin and you can feel it going down and you're trying to catch those lower lows.
The problem is, is that when markets do plummet, you just end up buying, buying, buying and eventually getting liquidated.
We can diversify, set stop rules and we can use AI responsibly to actually help us trade better.

strategy_components.description: They're simple trend following systems that I do and build here live on YouTube every single week.
I use simple tools like a Trendilio indicator for entries.
I use a T3 for trend direction.
And yes, they are all trend following, but they don't all enter at the same time.
They don't have the same stop losses and they certainly don't have the same way of taking profit.
They don't use the same logic and I'm very careful not to stack risk and I do my best to spread it.
In December, my account dropped by 11%, which was extreme pain for myself.
We went back into a trending market where Bitcoin, unfortunately for those holding it, absolutely dropped all the way down to almost $60,000.

explicit_conditions: They don't use the same logic and I'm very careful not to stack risk and I do my best to spread it.
